message:
fix: stop removing flock files to preserve concurrent lock protection (gt-vm7g2)

Removing a flock file while another process waits on it causes the waiter
to acquire a lock on the deleted inode, which provides no mutual exclusion
against a new process that creates a fresh file at the same path. Flock
works on file descriptors, not paths, so the file should persist.

Removes os.Remove calls for shutdown.lock in both gt down (down.go) and
the daemon isShutdownInProgress check (daemon.go).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
